text: The surgeon begins transanal total mesorectal excision (TME) with a Lone Star retractor in place. The tumor is located two centimeters above the dentate line, and the rectal wall is incised circumferentially at the dentate line.
The surgeon places a purse-string suture on the proximal part of the rectum and begins dissection from the posterior aspect.

The robotic total mesorectal excision (TME) is performed, beginning with the high ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and inferior mesenteric vein (IMV). The surgeon then initiates the excision of the mesorectal tissue.
The surgeon dissects the posterior side of the rectum, followed by the anterior and lateral parts, using a robotic-assisted approach for total mesorectal excision.

The surgeon connects the transanal and robotic TME dissection planes, then removes the specimen through a five-centimeter abdominal incision. Subsequently, a side-to-end coloanal anastomosis is performed.
